body:
---
id: note-0076
date: 2025-10-23
topic: investment-memo
mentions: []
---

Spent most of today pressure-testing our thesis on the Verdant Compute memo before it goes to the full partnership next week. The core tension I keep circling back to: the team has genuinely impressive GPU cluster utilization numbers, but their cooling infrastructure story relies on a municipal water agreement that isn't fully executed yet. That's not a dealbreaker, but it needs to be a condition of close, not a footnote.

On the climate side, I'm actually more bullish than I expected to be after the site visit. Their waste-heat recapture design is elegant — the kind of engineering decision that signals the founders think in systems, not just specs.

I want to push for a lower entry valuation given the permitting risk, but I also don't want to lose the deal to a fund that moves faster and asks fewer questions. That's the permanent tension in this seat. Writing this down mostly to remind myself: the questions are the job. If we stop asking them, we're just writing checks.
